sweeney todd.example_clue: In 2005 Michael Cerveris was a real cut-up in 19th century London in this Sondheim title role

natasha.example_clue: She's in trouble in Moscow & needs help from Pierre in a show with great acting, great songs & a great comet

annie oakley.example_clue: This character sings "You Can't Get A Man With A Gun" in Act I but duets on "An Old-Fashioned Wedding" in Act II

oh, mary!.example_clue: In 2026 it was announced Maya Rudolph would make her Broadway debut as a ditzy first lady in this hit play

hedwig and the angry inch.example_clue: This "measured" musical about a troubled rock singer debuted Off-Broadway in 1998 & became a Broadway smash in 2014

the big lebowski.example_clue: John Turturro's Jesus bowls as the Gipsy Kings' cover of "Hotel California" plays in this Coen brothers film

the indigo girls.example_clue: In 2023 this duo's "Closer To Fine" was the sing-along of choice for driving in & out of Barbieland

zoolander.example_clue: "Wake Me Up Before You Go-Go" soundtracks a gasoline fight that goes wrong in this comedy

peter gabriel.example_clue: As Lloyd Dobler in "Say Anything...", John Cusack held the boombox over his head & blasted "In Your Eyes" by this Brit

\"singin' in the rain\".example_clue: Malcolm McDowell improvised his performance of this song in "A Clockwork Orange" because it was the only song he knew the words to

frida kahlo.example_clue: Sung in both Spanish & English, the opera "Frida" tells the story of this artist's tortured life

swan lake.example_clue: Ruffling some feathers, Matthew Bourne's modern take on this Tchaikovsky ballet features male dancers as the birds, not females

rodeo.example_clue: The original cast of this Aaron Copland ballet was headed by Agnes de Mille, who choreographed it & danced the part of the lead cowgirl

la traviata.example_clue: This tragic Verdi opera features the celebrated drinking song "Brindisi", heard here

(modest) mussorgsky.example_clue: "Khovanshchina" is an unfinished opera by this Russian composer whose first name in English means unpretentious

blue suede shoes.example_clue: Whether they're worn by Carl Perkins or Elvis, don't you even think about stepping on these title items

\"blue jean baby\".example_clue: Zach Bryan's song about a goodbye has this apparel-related title, also the first 3 words of "Tiny Dancer"

\"blue monday\".example_clue: In this song New Order asked the question "How does it feel to treat me like you do?"

\"blue velvet\".example_clue: Lana Del Rey's cover of this fabric-focused classic was used in a promotional video by H&M

\"bullet the blue sky\".example_clue: Bono said Ronald Reagan was the red-faced man "peelin' off those dollar bills" & "slappin' 'em down" in this U2 song

\"lean on me\".example_clue: This Bill Withers classic about dependability is used to promote Verizon

inxs.example_clue: "Don't Change" by this Aussie band is supposed to get you to buy a Chevy Equinox

the vines.example_clue: Brands from Apple to NASCAR used this band's song "Ride", as in "Ri-ide with me ho-o-ome"

\"like a rock\".example_clue: Bob Seger's dad worked at Ford forever, but this song of Bob's was used to sell Chevy trucks in a 13-year campaign

supergrass.example_clue: Arthritis drug Enbrel used an instrumental of this '90s band's "Alright"

\"umbrella\".example_clue: Rihanna topped the charts for 7 weeks in 2007 with this collab

\"uptown girl\".example_clue: Billy Joel said he began writing this song while dating Elle Macpherson but ended up making it about Christie Brinkley

unholy.example_clue: An irreverent 2022 chart topper by Sam Smith & Kim Petras said, "Daddy's getting hot at the body shop doing something" this

\"under pressure\".example_clue: Brian May said John Deacon's bass riff was the starting point for this collaboration with David Bowie

\"unchained melody\".example_clue: This Righteous Brothers classic about love was originally written for a 1955 prison movie, hence the title

red.example_clue: Taylor Swift sang, "Losing him was blue like I'd never known / Missing him was dark gray, all alone... but loving him was" this

blackpink.example_clue: "Born Pink" by this K-pop quartet debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard Albums chart in 2022

green day.example_clue: They probably had the time of their lives opening Super Bowl 60 with a set that included "Holiday"

\"yellow\".example_clue: Look at the stars! In 2024 this Coldplay hit passed 1 billion views on YouTube

ac/dc.example_clue: "Back to Black" is a 2006 album by Amy Winehouse; "Back in Black" is a 1980 release from these Aussie rockers

mercury.example_clue: In Holst's "The Planets", this section is subtitled "The Winged Messenger"

the messiah.example_clue: The text of this Handel oratorio was arranged by Charles Jennens from the Bible; Isaiah supplied "for unto us a child is born"

nietzsche.example_clue: Richard Strauss' "Also Sprach Zarathustra" was a nod to this countryman

elgar.example_clue: King Charles' 2023 coronation featured pomp, circumstance & music too, like from this Brit who was knighted in 1904

liszt.example_clue: In 1840 this Hungarian composer created the modern piano recital, playing scores from memory

the who.example_clue: For the song "Baba O'Riley", Pete Townshend of this band recalled the "desolation... at Woodstock"

\"hello\".example_clue: Adele said that this song was a "real ode" to the "little me" & "older me" -- perhaps from "the other side"

morissette.example_clue: This singer said, "It is ironic that a song called 'Ironic' isn't filled with ironies"

\"good riddance\".example_clue: After his GF left him for the streets of Ecuador, Billie Joe Armstrong wrote this song -- "I hope you had the time of your life"

\"forgot about dre\".example_clue: Eminem rhymes "walking by" with "caught a guy", "an awkward eye" & "Karl Kani" in this song about those who neglect Andre Young

\"smelly cat\".example_clue: Taylor Swift has purrformed this folk ditty by Phoebe Buffay

parks and rec.example_clue: On this sitcom Leslie Knope goes to Washington & learns that Cory Booker & Orrin Hatch are in a folk band called Across the Isle

(john c.) reilly.example_clue: This actor in "Walk Hard" briefly goes folk with bizarre Dylanesque lyrics like "Cabbage is the darling of the laundromat"

a mighty wind.example_clue: "Never Did No Wanderin'" is a song by The Folksmen , a fauxk band that gets back together in this Christopher Guest mockumentary

carey mulligan.example_clue: A folk duo reunites in "The Ballad of Wallis Island", a film starring this actress; IRL she's married to folk's Marcus Mumford

quintets.example_clue: Brahms' string these have an extra viola to get the odd number of players; Schubert doubles down on the cello

debussy.example_clue: This French composer known for evocative piano works only left us one movement of his one symphony from 1880

judas maccabaeus.example_clue: "See, The Conqu'ring Hero Comes" is heard in Handel's oratorio about this title hero, often performed around Hanukkah

dvořák.example_clue: Nominated for a 2026 Grammy, the guitar duo album "Slavic Sessions" includes 2 of this Czech composer's "Slavonic Dances"

the carnival of the animals.example_clue: This work by Saint-Saëns is also called a "Zoological Fantasy"

a minuet.example_clue: Brahms was just one composer of this type of piece with a name from the French for "small" & also a dance

lieder.example_clue: Before his death at 31, Schubert composed 600 songs called these in German

a nocturne.example_clue: Manuel de Falla's "Nights in the Gardens of Spain" is a set of this type of composition, named for the time of day mentioned

an opera buffa.example_clue: Rossini would have called a comic opera this in Italian; his first, "The Bill of Marriage", came out in 1810

the polonaise.example_clue: Chopin was famed for his "heroic" one of these , named for the country of his birth

an apron.example_clue: Walmart sells one you can wear that says "kiss the chef"

buy myself flowers.example_clue: Miley Cyrus sings, "I can" do this, "write my name in the sand"

usher.example_clue: This single-named singer headlined the 2024 Super Bowl halftime show

golden.example_clue: A very popular tune by HUNTR/X says, "You know together we're glowing, gonna be, gonna be" this

a box.example_clue: What kids play with instead of the fancy, expensive toy

\"johnny b. goode\".example_clue: This rock & roll classic says, "Never ever learned to read or write so well but... could play a guitar just like a-ringin' a bell"

a stage.example_clue: A segment of the Tour de France; one can be hilly or flat

\"born this way\".example_clue: This title ends the Lady Gaga lyric "I'm beautiful in my way 'cause God makes no mistakes, I'm on the right track, baby, I was..."

prop.example_clue: Shortened term for the rotating blades on a plane that make it go

the fate of ophelia.example_clue: A No. 1 hit by Taylor Swift says, "You dug me out of my grave & saved my heart from" this, a Shakespeare reference

a chorus line.example_clue: "I Hope I Get It" & "Dance: Ten, Looks: Three" typify the hunger in this show

come from away.example_clue: Joel Goldes coached the cast in Newfoundland dialect for this musical

operation mincemeat.example_clue: A top-secret plan to deceive Nazis using a corpse is out in the open as a Colin Firth movie & this Olivier- & Tony-winning musical

starlight express.example_clue: In 1987, the Gershwin Theater was transformed into a roller rink for this elaborate musical

brigadoon.example_clue: Angus MacGuffie is a character in this musical about a disappearing & reappearing town

sinatra.example_clue: He sang, "I did it my way", & my way to get to the N.J. rest area named for him is to head north past the Atlantic City reservoir

the chicago symphony orchestra.example_clue: Beyoncé's 32nd Grammy broke the record of Sir Georg Solti, longtime music director of this Illinoisy group, the CSO

yodeling.example_clue: Best known for this singing technique, Jimmie Rodgers was in the 1st class of inductees to the Country Music Hall of Fayiyayayiyame

the ronettes.example_clue: Hal Blaine played drums on 150 Top 10 singles, including his classic opening on this group's "Be My Baby"

miles davis.example_clue: The 2026 centennial of this trumpeter is planned to include reissues, multimedia shows, & a film on his romance with Juliette Gréco

star trek.example_clue: Eastman School of Music alum Alexander Courage boldly went & composed the theme for this TV show; Gene Roddenberry wrote unused lyrics

tchaikovsky.example_clue: The Moscow State Conservatory is named for this composer whose first symphony was 1866's "Winter Daydreams"

john williams.example_clue: Flying off from the Air Force in 1954, he studied piano at Juilliard for a bit before moving his wars to the stars & scoring a few films

the berklee school of music.example_clue: Sounding like a West Coast university, this Boston school of music offers 15 majors including jazz composition & game scoring

anderson.example_clue: The symphony orchestra at the tuition-free Curtis Institute of Music plays in a hall named for this Black opera diva
